description: Definitions and use cases for the cleaning methods relevant to SunSweeper services.

pressure_washing.name: Pressure Washing
pressure_washing.description: A cleaning method that uses pressurized water for durable surfaces that can safely handle it.
not_appropriate_for: Materials that can be etched, lifted, or damaged
key_points: Method choice depends on surface type
More pressure is not always better

soft_washing.name: Soft Washing
soft_washing.description: A low pressure chemical cleaning approach used on more delicate surfaces where aggressive pressure could cause damage.
not_appropriate_for: Situations where only high pressure would remove embedded buildup from durable flatwork
very dirty roofs of any material type as it will only bake on the dead organic material
key_points: Chosen when the surface needs a gentler method
Often used for stucco home cleaning work

purified_water_cleaning.name: Purified Water Cleaning
purified_water_cleaning.description: A cleaning method that uses purified water to help clean surfaces without leaving hard water spotting.
appropriate_for: Surfaces where spotting is a concern
key_points: Commonly used in solar panel cleaning
Supports a cleaner finish on glass like surfaces

hydro_jet_flushing.name: Hydro Jet Flushing
hydro_jet_flushing.description: A focused water based flushing method used to break up and move sediment, roots, and debris from narrow enclosed channels.
appropriate_for: Narrow debris packed drainage channels where access is limited
key_points: Useful for restoring flow in enclosed gutter systems
Effective where hand cleaning alone is difficult
Best used with judgment based on the condition of the system

manual_debris_removal.name: Manual Debris Removal
manual_debris_removal.description: Physical removal of debris by hand or hand tools where direct extraction is needed before flushing or finishing.
appropriate_for: Areas where material must be physically pulled out before water flow can be restored
key_points: Useful when roots or compacted sediment are present

solar_panels.customer_friendly_answer: Solar panels should be cleaned with solar safe methods, not blasted with high pressure.

roofs.customer_friendly_answer: The right roof cleaning method depends on the roofing material and soiling level. A proper evaluation matters.

concrete.customer_friendly_answer: Concrete and metal are often a good candidates for pressure washing because they are durable surfaces.

mobile_home_carport_gutters.customer_friendly_answer: Those enclosed carport gutters often need specialty clearing because they pack full of dirt, roots, and plant growth.
